task_completion.visual_evidence: Gretl main window displays the dataset variables (invest_ge, value_ge, capital_ge, invest_wh, value_wh, capital_wh) or a script/console shows the command 'open grunfeld.gdt'.

task_completion.description: Define the two equations for the SUR system
task_completion.visual_evidence: A system specification dialog, script editor, or output window showing Equation 1 with dependent variable 'invest_ge' and regressors 'const', 'value_ge', 'capital_ge', and Equation 2 with dependent variable 'invest_wh' and regressors 'const', 'value_wh', 'capital_wh'.

task_completion.description: Run the Seemingly Unrelated Regressions (SUR) estimation
task_completion.visual_evidence: An output window showing 'Seemingly Unrelated Regressions' or 'SUR' estimation results, including coefficients, standard errors, and statistics for both the GE and WH equations.

task_completion.description: Save the full estimation output to the specified text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save as' dialog with the path '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/sur_results.txt', a script command saving to this path, or a terminal/text editor showing the saved file contents at that exact path.

integrity.description: The agent used Gretl (GUI or script editor) to perform the estimation
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Gretl application interface, menus, or console, rather than just typing text into a generic editor.

integrity.description: The estimation output contains full, authentic statistical details
integrity.visual_evidence: The output includes standard errors, t-ratios, p-values, R-squared, and cross-equation correlation, rather than just a manually typed list of coefficients.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply copy-paste expected answers
integrity.visual_evidence: The results are generated by the software's estimation engine, formatted in Gretl's standard output style, and not manually constructed.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses the authentic Grunfeld (1958) investment dataset. The expected SUR coefficients are: General Electric value_ge ~0.038 (range 0.02-0.06), capital_ge ~0.139 (range 0.05-0.25); Westinghouse value_wh ~0.058 (range 0.03-0.09), capital_wh ~0.064 (range 0.01-0.20). The cross-equation correlation between GE and WH equations in SUR estimation is approximately 0.72896. The VLM should look for these values in the estimation output to verify correctness.
